title: Web storage OPFS save import export and quota gate
summary: Validate Web storage, OPFS, save/load, export/import and quota failure behavior for the M6 product gate.
acceptance: OPFS save, load, export, import and quota-failure recovery are tested
Windows/Web save container interoperability is proven or recorded as a product-gate blocker
Error states produce user-facing and diagnostic structured reasons
No public save compatibility promise is made beyond the M6 gate

history.details: Instantiated from reviewed WM-0084 M6 DAG; keep proposed until dependencies are done.

objective: Validate Web storage and save/import/export behavior required for product-gate decision evidence.
forbiddenPaths: public save compatibility promise beyond M6 gate
risksAndFileOwnership: Risk: browser quota APIs are inconsistent.
Risk: save export may expose local paths or sensitive diagnostics.
File ownership is limited to persistence, platform and web storage UI surfaces.
ownerGate: Block on public save migration, codec change or compatibility commitment without ADR
Block on local path, secret or full-save leakage in diagnostics
Block if platform storage mutates authoritative simulation state
m0M5RegressionCoverage: Save/load changes must preserve M5 focused save/replay evidence or provide reviewed migration evidence
Simulation Worker remains the only world writer
benchmarkImpact: May add storage timing or quota evidence; does not update M0-M5 benchmark baselines.
reviewRouting: client-engineer completes to reviewer; reviewer verifies persistence/security boundaries.
rollbackModel: Revert Web storage gate changes and leave product decision blocked on storage evidence.

sparkEligibility.reason: Web persistence, save/export and quota behavior touch product and security boundaries.
